tests.label: CFT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a small ASCII text payload using the primary non-deprecated overload with an explicit dataTypeDescriptor of 'text/plain; charset=UTF-8'. Verifies the method returns true and the data can be read back.
tests.notes: Exercises the primary non-deprecated overload; verifies MIME type is accepted and round-trip read-back matches.

tests.label: CFT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Call the primary overload with dataTypeDescriptor=null. Verifies that the client defaults to 'application/octet-stream' internally and the write succeeds without a NullPointerException.
tests.notes: Validates the null-guard on dataTypeDescriptor in HPCCWsFileIOClient.

tests.label: CFT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Call the primary overload with dataTypeDescriptor='' (empty string). Verifies the internal isEmpty() guard defaults to 'application/octet-stream' and the write succeeds.
tests.notes: Exercises the isEmpty() branch of the MIME type null-guard.

tests.label: CFT-004-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a byte array containing non-printable binary bytes (simulated PNG header) and verify round-trip correctness via readFileData. Validates MTOM/MIME encoding preserves binary content.
tests.notes: Uses ISO-8859-1 for byte comparison since readFileData returns a Java String.

tests.label: CFT-005-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a JSON string with dataTypeDescriptor='application/json' and verify the server accepts it. Tests client-side DataSource construction with a non-standard MIME type.
tests.notes: Server-side storage is MIME-agnostic; primarily tests client DataSource construction.

tests.label: CFT-006-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a 100-byte payload with uploadchunksize=20, forcing 5 loop iterations. Verifies the full data is written correctly via readFileData length check.
tests.notes: Directly exercises the while(bytesleft > 0) loop for multiple iterations.

tests.label: CFT-007-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass uploadchunksize=0 and uploadchunksize=-1 to verify the client substitutes defaultUploadChunkSize (5,000,000) and writes succeed without arithmetic errors.
tests.notes: Two writes in one test method (cft007a and cft007b); both must return true.

tests.label: CFT-008-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Create a file, write known content with chunksize=5 (multiple chunks), read it back, and assert byte-level equality. Definitive correctness end-to-end test.
tests.notes: Small chunksize=5 exercises multi-chunk correctness and data ordering simultaneously.

tests.label: ECT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Set uploadchunksize exactly equal to data.length so the loop executes exactly once. Verifies correct single-chunk boundary behaviour.
tests.notes: Boundary condition: chunksize == data.length == 10 bytes.

tests.label: ECT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Set uploadchunksize larger than data.length (chunk exceeds payload). Loop runs exactly once; verifies no ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
tests.notes: 9-byte data with chunksize=100.

tests.label: ECT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write a single-byte payload (0x42). Verifies the minimum valid non-empty write is handled correctly.
tests.notes: Minimum boundary for non-empty data; uploadchunksize=1.

tests.label: ECT-004-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: In a bare-metal environment, provide an explicit lzAddress from system property. Verify the client sets DestNetAddress in the request and the write succeeds.
tests.notes: Skipped in containerized environments via assumeFalse(isContainerized).

tests.label: ECT-005-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Write approximately 1 MB of data with a 100 KB chunk size (~10 iterations). Verify all bytes are written without timeout or exception.
tests.notes: Tests chunked loop performance. Optional readFileData confirmation omitted due to ESP response size limits.

tests.label: ECT-006-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass append=false and document that the client overrides this to append=true in the loop (Note A bug). Both writes produce concatenated content 'FIRSTSECOND' (11 bytes).
tests.notes: Intentional bug documentation test. When bug is fixed, expected read-back should be 'SECOND' (6 bytes) and test will need updating.

tests.label: ECT-007-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass offset=5 and document that the client loop overrides it to data.length (Note A bug). Write still succeeds since append=true causes the server to ignore Offset.
tests.notes: Intentional bug documentation test; exists as a regression marker for Note A offset override.

tests.label: EHT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass data=null. Expect NullPointerException at data.length before any server call is made.
tests.notes: Client-side exception; no network call made.

tests.label: EHT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass data=new byte[0]. bytesleft=0 so the loop never runs; method returns true without any server communication. Documents silent success edge case.
tests.notes: Loop body never entered when data is empty; no server call made.

tests.label: EHT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass fileName=null. Server returns 'Destination path not specified'; client does not detect this as an error and returns true. Documents client-side bug.
tests.notes: Intentional client bug documentation. If bug is fixed, expect false instead of true.

tests.label: EHT-004-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass fileName='' (empty string). Same server-side validation failure as EHT-003; client returns true due to same bug.
tests.notes: Parallel to EHT-003; exercises empty-string path.

tests.label: EHT-005-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass targetLandingZone=null. Server returns 'Destination not specified'; client returns true (bug).
tests.notes: Documents server-side error not surfaced as client-side failure.

tests.label: EHT-006-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Pass targetLandingZone='' (empty string). Server returns 'Destination not specified'; client returns true (bug).
tests.notes: Parallel to EHT-005.

tests.label: EHT-007-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Call writeHPCCFileData without first calling createHPCCFile. Server returns '<path> does not exist.'; client returns true (bug).
tests.notes: Documents that the client silently ignores 'file not found'. If bug is fixed, expect false.

tests.label: EHT-008-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Provide invalid lzAddress '999.999.999.999' in a bare-metal environment. Server's validateDropZoneAccess should reject the request.
tests.notes: Skipped in containerized environments via assumeFalse(isContainerized).

tests.label: EHT-009-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Construct an HPCCWsFileIOClient with an invalid host/port and call writeHPCCFileData. Expect an Exception from verifyStub() or the network layer.
tests.notes: Uses HPCCWsFileIOClient.get() factory with 'invalid.host.invalid:9999'.

tests.label: EHT-010-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Attempt to write using a client with deliberately wrong credentials on a security-enabled cluster. Server should reject with an authorization error.
tests.notes: Skipped via assumeTrue(client.doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate()) on unsecured clusters.

tests.label: CNT-001-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Confirm the WsFileIO service is reachable by executing a valid minimal writeHPCCFileData call. No AxisFault, ConnectException, or UnknownHostException should be thrown.
tests.notes: Distinct from existing ping test — exercises the full WriteFileData SOAP operation path.

tests.label: CNT-002-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Confirm that a write request using the configured valid credentials succeeds on a security-enabled cluster.
tests.notes: Skipped via assumeTrue(client.doesTargetHPCCAuthenticate()) on unsecured clusters.

tests.label: CNT-003-writeHPCCFileData
tests.description: Confirm that a write request with bad credentials is rejected at the authentication layer on a security-enabled cluster.
tests.notes: Equivalent to EHT-010 from auth perspective; included for connectivity matrix completeness.
